task: Investigate this Windows host (the ROCBA intrusion). Work these steps in order and do NOT skip any: (1) call get_timestomp_candidates then detect_timestomps; (2) call detect_masquerade; (3) call get_remote_access (local vs remote, source IPs, outbound pivot); (4) call get_usb_devices (removable-media exfil); (5) call get_web_activity (browser downloads / exfil to USB / SharePoint sources); (6) call get_program_execution (exfil tooling, shells, anti-forensics). Only AFTER all six steps, output a FINDINGS report covering the disk/memory contradictions AND the real-case exfiltration kill chain, citing artifacts (IPs, USB serials, URLs, file paths, MFT entries, PIDs).

rounds.assistant:
**FINDINGS – ROCBA Intrusion (Windows 10 host)** --- ### 1. Disk‑/Memory Contradictions | # | Artifact (type) | Disk evidence (MFT / $SI‑$FN) | Memory evidence (PID / start‑time / path) | Contradiction | Technique | |---|-----------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|---------------|-----------| | 1 | **OneDrive.exe** (executable) | MFT #104046 – $SI crtime = 2019‑03‑15 08:00 Z, $FN crtime = 2020‑10‑27 02:56 Z (Δ ≈ 592 days) – high‑confidence timestomp candidate. | Running process PID 9648, start 2020‑11‑11 08:14 02 Z, image path `users/fredr/appdata/local/microsoft/onedrive/onedrive.exe`. | The file’s creation time in $SI is forged (back‑dated) while the true $FN time matches the actual execution. | **Timestomp (T1070.006)** – corroborated by live process. | | 2 | **svchost.exe** (binary) | MFT #104210 – name “svchost.exe” located at `users/fredr/appdata/local/microsoft/onedrive/svchost.exe` (outside System32/SysWOW64). | No matching process in memory (PID null). | Binary masquerades as a legitimate system service but resides in a user‑writable profile directory. | **Masquerade (T1036)** – disk‑only evidence, but the absence of a legitimate system path is a clear red flag. | *All other high‑confidence timestomp candidates (Adobe setup.exe, DiffMatchPatch.dll) have **no** corresponding live process and are therefore treated as non‑executed artifacts.* --- ### 2. Intrusion & Exfiltration Narrative | Aspect | Evidence (artifact) | Interpretation | |--------|---------------------|----------------| | **Remote access – initial compromise** | Terminal Services logs show a **remote RDP logon** at `2020‑11‑14 03:42:50 Z` (event 25) from source IP **52.249.198.56** (remote = true). Six remote sessions total (IP 52.249.198.56). | The attacker gained a foothold via RDP from an external host. | | **Outbound RDP pivot (lateral movement)** | Two outbound RDP client connections recorded at `2020‑11‑14 05:00:50 Z` and `05:05:37 Z` to internal address **172.16.6.18** (EID 1102). | The compromised host was used to attempt lateral movement toward another internal system. | | **USB removable‑media usage** | USB device **serial 058F63666438** (“Multiple Card Reader”) first installed `2020‑11‑02 17:19:44 Z`, last arrived `2020‑11‑10 14:21:21 Z`, mounted as drive **F:**. | This device was present when the user copied data to the removable drive. | | **Web‑download exfiltration to USB** | Edge download record (exfil = true) – `2020‑11‑14 04:13:22 Z`, source URL **https://starkresearchlabs‑my.sharepoint.com/.../Quantum%20Particles%20Affected%20by%20Other%20Dimensions.pdf**, saved to **F:\Files from SRL system\Quantum Particles Affected by Other Dimensions.pdf**. | Sensitive research file was deliberately downloaded from a corporate SharePoint location directly onto the USB drive (serial 058F63666438). | | **Additional exfil‑related downloads** | – `2020‑10‑30 19:23:02 Z` (SharePoint zip) saved to `C:\Users\fredr\Downloads\WorkingFiles.zip` (exfil = true).
– `2020‑10‑28 12:25:12 Z` & `2020‑10‑29 02:18:23 Z` (Dropbox installers) flagged as exfil. | Multiple files from cloud services were staged for exfiltration, indicating a systematic data‑theft effort. | | **Program execution supporting exfil** | *GoogleDriveFSSetup.exe* executed at `2020‑11‑10 14:12:01 Z` (UserAssist entry, path **F:\GoogleDriveFSSetup.exe**).
*cmd.exe* run at `2020‑11‑14 12:43:01 Z`.
*DropboxUninstaller.exe* run at `2020‑11‑14 13:50:02 Z`. | Execution of cloud‑sync client installers and command‑shell activity aligns with the attacker preparing and using removable media for data transfer. | | **Brute‑force background noise** | 60 failed logon events (4625) from external IPs (e.g., 213.202.233.104) on `2020‑11‑15`. | Indicates opportunistic scanning/brute‑force but not directly tied to the successful RDP compromise. | #### Consolidated Kill‑Chain 1. **Initial RDP compromise** – attacker connects from **52.249.198.56** (remote logon). 2. **Persistence / anti‑forensics** – the attacker runs a forged **OneDrive.exe** (timestomped) from the user profile, masking its true creation time. 3. **Credential/privilege escalation** – command‑shell (`cmd.exe`) and cloud‑sync tools (Google Drive, Dropbox) are launched, likely to stage data. 4. **Data staging** – sensitive PDFs are downloaded from SharePoint directly onto the removable USB (drive F:, serial 058F63666438). 5. **Exfiltration** – the USB device is later removed (last removal `2020‑11‑10 14:23:33 Z`), carrying the stolen files. 6. **Lateral movement attempt** – outbound RDP connections to internal host **172.16.6.18** suggest the attacker tried to move laterally after exfiltration. --- ### Summary - **Confirmed anti‑forensic actions:** - *OneDrive.exe* – timestomped (MFT #104046, PID 9648). - *svchost.exe* – masqueraded binary (MFT #104210). - **Compromise vector:** Remote RDP from **52.249.198.56**. - **Exfiltration path:** SharePoint‑hosted PDFs downloaded to USB device **058F63666438** (drive F:), corroborated by Edge download logs and UserAssist execution of Google Drive setup. - **Lateral movement:** Outbound RDP attempts to **172.16.6.18**. These artifacts collectively demonstrate a coordinated intrusion: initial remote access, deliberate anti‑forensic masking, staging of valuable data on removable media, and an attempted pivot to other internal assets.

– `2020‑10‑28 12:25:12 Z` & `2020‑10‑29 02:18:23 Z` (Dropbox installers) flagged as exfil. | Multiple files from cloud services were staged for exfiltration, indicating a systematic data‑theft effort. | | **Program execution supporting exfil** | *GoogleDriveFSSetup.exe* executed at `2020‑11‑10 14:12:01 Z` (UserAssist entry, path **F:\GoogleDriveFSSetup.exe**).
